vulnerability.reference: Benchmark designs assume independence of test cases and lack inter-agent contagion monitoring.
vulnerability.generated: Evaluation protocols lack mechanisms to detect cascading jailbreak infections, blinding safety assessments to exponential multi-agent compromise.

impact.reference: Unsafe models incorrectly deemed secure, leading to premature deployment with hidden vulnerabilities.
impact.generated: Safety researchers deploy infected multi-agent systems under false confidence, causing all agents to exhibit coordinated harmful behaviors.

dose_response.reference: Infectious propagation of jailbreak prompts via adversarial images in multi-agent red-teaming leads to underestimating safety risks, causing premature deployment of insecure models and wasted evaluation resources.
dose_response.generated: Jailbreak prompts propagating infectiously among multimodal LLM agents via adversarial images, through safety researchers testing multi-agent systems, triggers evaluation protocols lacking cascading infection detection, leading to deployment of infected agents under false confidence and coordinated harmful behaviors.
